data.title: XRP News Today: XRP Down Nearly 2%, Underperforms Wider Crypto Market
data.description: Key TakeawaysXRP price drops over 2%, lagging a broadly positive crypto marketBearish technical structure, with XRP trading below all major moving averagesWhale transfers and ETF uncertainty add to short-term selling pressurePrice Slides Amid Rotation Into Higher-Momentum AssetsXRP traded near $2.13, down close to 2%, even as several leading cryptocurrencies posted gains. Analysts note a visible rotation into high-momentum assets such as Solana, Ethereum and meme-tokens, drawing speculative capital away from slower-moving coins. This shift has pressured XRP’s short-term performance and reinforced a trend of relative weakness against the broader crypto market. Network Activity Declines and Sparks Utility ConcernsA sharp pullback in XRP’s network activity, following prior transactional spikes, has raised questions around organic demand. Analysts argue that abrupt swings in transactions can reflect synthetic activity rather than real economic usage. For sentiment to improve, traders are watching for consistent daily transactions to signal renewed network utility. Technical Indicators Turn Bearish Across All Moving AveragesXRP’s technical outlook remains weak. The token sits below every major moving average, including the SMA-20 at $2.1388, SMA-50 at $2.3050, and SMA-200 at $2.6137. All listed moving averages reflect a bearish alignment, indicating continued downward pressure. Momentum also remains soft, consistent with a market favoring cautious positioning and low confidence in short-term upside. Whale Transfers and Regulatory Delays Add Mixed SignalsOn-chain data shows large XRP transfers between major exchange wallets, which traders interpret as possible liquidity shifts and increased circulating supply. At the same time, interest in XRP ETFs remains steady, though regulatory timelines continue to lag behind other large-cap digital assets. According to analyst Viktoras Karapetjanc, regulatory stagnation and weak on-chain engagement continue to weigh on XRP’s sentiment relative to peers. Outlook: Can XRP Reclaim Short-Term Levels?For XRP to regain momentum, analysts are watching whether it can stabilize above the SMA-20 and push toward prior short-term levels near $2.20. A rebound in network activity, along with clearer progress in institutional-grade products, would be needed to improve XRP’s market alignment. Until then, the coin may continue to lag higher-momentum digital assets during rapid market cycles.

data.title: Pepe Website Compromised by Cyber Attack
data.description: According to PANews, the official website of the meme coin PEPE has been compromised by attackers, redirecting users to malicious links. On Thursday, cybersecurity firm Blockaid reported that their system detected a front-end attack on the Pepe website, which now contains malicious code known as Inferno Drainer. Inferno Drainer is a toolkit used by threat actors, comprising phishing site templates, wallet-stealing programs, and social engineering tools.

data.title: Lion Group Holding to Acquire Bitcoin with Convertible Bond Proceeds
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Lion Group Holding Ltd., a company listed on the Nasdaq, has announced the signing of an amendment to its securities purchase agreement. The company aims to raise a total of $9.984 million through convertible bonds. Of this amount, $8 million in net proceeds is earmarked for the acquisition of Bitcoin (BTC) to be used as corporate treasury reserves.

data.title: U.S. Stock Markets Open with Gains on December 4
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the Nasdaq Composite Index opened on December 4 with an increase of 36.71 points, marking a 0.16% rise to 23,490.8 points. The S&P 500 Index also saw an uptick, gaining 12.44 points, or 0.18%, to reach 6,862.16 points. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones Industrial Average opened with a rise of 99.41 points, representing a 0.21% increase, bringing it to 47,982.31 points.

data.title: Binance Blockchain Week 2025 Concludes with Key Insights and Debates
data.description:
Binance Blog published a new article, marking the conclusion of Binance Blockchain Week 2025, a major event in the cryptocurrency industry. The second day of the event wrapped up with engaging discussions on the future of digital assets, payments, and Web3 infrastructure. Thousands of participants, including builders, thinkers, policymakers, and creators, gathered at Dubai’s Coca-Cola Arena for two days of high-impact conversations and market insights. The event highlighted the industry's accelerating maturation and the innovations poised to drive real-world adoption in 2026 and beyond.

Day 2 featured a much-anticipated debate between Binance’s co-founder, Changpeng Zhao (CZ), and renowned investor Peter Schiff on Bitcoin versus tokenized gold. Schiff argued for tokenized gold as a timeless store of value, while CZ defended Bitcoin's trustless design and global community. The debate underscored the shifting narrative in the industry, with digital scarcity gaining traction over traditional gold maximalism. Additionally, Raoul Pal, Co-founder & CEO of Real Vision, shared his macro thesis for 2026, emphasizing the importance of avoiding FOMO and holding quality assets. He likened altcoins to small-cap equities, suggesting they offer significant upside potential.

The event also explored the fusion of prediction markets, media, and DeFi into a more participatory financial layer. Panelists discussed how real-world data can now be surfaced instantly through on-chain prediction markets, allowing for real-time updates and monetization of insights. Leaders from Mastercard, Ripple, and TON examined how blockchain and stablecoins are modernizing payments, stressing the importance of compliance and user safety. The discussions highlighted the growing relevance of digital assets in the next generation of payment systems.

The closing note by Yi He, Binance’s Co-CEO, emphasized the importance of confidence and safety in using crypto technology. The event concluded with a focus on real-world applications, including how digital assets are transforming property, payments, identity, and data networks. The Innovation Stage featured insights from top traders and discussions on Web2 and Web3 marketing, showcasing the industry's convergence with global finance. As Binance Blockchain Week 2025 comes to an end, the momentum continues to build for the next era of Web3, with the industry’s brightest minds shaping the future of digital assets.

data.title: Thai Authorities Seize $8.6 Million in Bitcoin Mining Equipment Linked to Fraud Syndicates
data.description: According to PANews, Thai authorities have confiscated Bitcoin mining equipment valued at $8.6 million (300 million baht) from seven locations suspected of funding transnational fraud syndicates operating in Myanmar. On Tuesday, the Department of Special Investigation conducted raids at six sites in Samut Sakhon Province and one in Uthai Thani Province, seizing 3,642 mining devices worth $7.7 million (270 million baht) and electrical equipment valued at $860,000 (30 million baht). Investigators discovered that most of the mining equipment was installed in soundproof containers equipped with water cooling systems. These sites are linked to a fraud syndicate based in Myanmar, with financial transactions totaling over $143 million (5 billion baht).

data.title: First Leveraged SUI ETF Launched on Nasdaq
data.description: According to PANews, 21Shares has introduced the first leveraged SUI ETF, named '2x Leveraged SUI ETF' (Ticker: TXXS), on Nasdaq following approval from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). This marks the debut of an ETF linked to the Sui ecosystem and is among the few such products across blockchain networks. TXXS offers investors leveraged exposure to Sui through a familiar, regulated ETF structure. This launch follows news of 21Shares' plans to introduce a spot SUI ETF, which is currently under SEC review.

data.title: AlphaTON Capital Files $420.69 Million Shelf Registration to Boost AI and HPC Expansion
data.description:
According to Foresight News, AlphaTON Capital, a Nasdaq-listed company, has submitted a shelf registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for $420.69 million. This move aims to enhance the company's financing flexibility to support future expansion projects in artificial intelligence and high-performance computing infrastructure.

The shelf registration statement, authorized under U.S. securities law, allows issuers to offer securities over a specified period, typically three years, in multiple rounds. This method enables companies to strategically time their offerings based on market conditions and funding needs.

In addition to infrastructure expansion, AlphaTON Capital plans to pursue strategic mergers and acquisitions within profitable companies in the Telegram ecosystem.

data.title: Android Chip Vulnerability Poses Risk to Web3 Wallets, Researchers Warn
data.description:
According to Foresight News, researchers at Ledger have identified a vulnerability in Android chips that could lead to devices being fully controlled, posing a risk to smartphone-based Web3 wallets from physical attacks. The Donjon research team at Ledger discovered that hardware fault injection can bypass core security checks and take complete control of the chip. The company emphasized that this finding does not affect Ledger hardware wallets but highlights the risks of relying solely on smartphone hot wallets for digital asset protection.

The Donjon team detailed in their report how they detected the vulnerability in MediaTek's Dimensity 7300 (MT6878) chip, which is widely used in many Android consumer phones. Ledger stated that the research confirms that even the most advanced smartphone chips remain susceptible to physical attacks, making them unsuitable for safeguarding private keys. The vulnerability was disclosed to MediaTek in May. Ledger noted that MediaTek has responded positively and has informed affected manufacturers.

data.title: Cloudflare just stopped the largest DDoS attack ever
data.description:
Cloudflare announced that it had detected and stopped the 29. 7-terabit-per-second (Tbps) distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack, which was the biggest one ever. According to the web infrastructure and security company, the activity originated from a DDoS botnet-for-hire known as AISURU. It has been linked to several hyper-volumetric DDoS attacks over the past year. The attack lasted […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/cloudflare-stop-largest-ddos-attack/
